messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
What is the total number of games played in the National League Championship Series and World Series that the St. Louis Cardinals won for which game summaries are available

content.message:
I need to find the total number of games played in the National League Championship Series (NLCS) and World Series that the St. Louis Cardinals won, where game summaries are available.

Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to identify the St. Louis Cardinals entity in Wikidata
2. Then find the sports events (NLCS and World Series) that the Cardinals won
3. Finally, count the total number of games played in those series where summaries are available

Let me start by searching for the St. Louis Cardinals entity:

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Arizona Cardinals (wd:Q224164, matched via "St. Louis Cardinals"), also known as "Chicago Cardinals", "Morgan Athletic Club", "Phoenix Cardinals", "Racine Cardinals", "St. Louis Cardinals":
  - is instance of American football team
  - National Football League franchise in Glendale, Arizona
2. St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q504309), also known as "Browns", "Brown Stockings", "Cardinals", "Cards", "Perfectos", etc.:
  - is instance of baseball team
  - Major League Baseball franchise in St. Louis, Missouri
3. St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q2938063):
  - history of the football team now known as the Arizona Cardinals when in St. Louis
  - is instance of American football team
4. St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q12002552):
  - is instance of Wikimedia disambiguation page
  - Wikimedia disambiguation page
5. Category:St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q8809299):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
6. Template:St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q14338185):
  - is instance of Wikimedia template
  - Wikimedia template
7. St. Louis Cardinal (wd:Q110248988):
  - is instance of Wikimedia disambiguation page
  - Wikimedia disambiguation page
8. history of the St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q16980314, matched via "St. Louis Cardinals history"), also known as "St. Louis Cardinals history", "the St. Louis Cardinals history":
  - aspect of history
  - is instance of aspect of history
9. history of the St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q16980317, matched via "St. Louis Cardinals history"), also known as "St. Louis Cardinals history", "the St. Louis Cardinals history":
  - is instance of aspect of history
  - sports Team History
10. history of the St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q16980321, matched via "St. Louis Cardinals history"), also known as "St. Louis Cardinals history", "the St. Louis Cardinals history":
  - is instance of aspect of history

content.message:
I need to clarify which St. Louis Cardinals we're referring to. The query mentions "National League Championship Series and World Series" which are baseball series, not football. 

Looking at the results, option 2 is clearly the baseball team "St. Louis Cardinals" which plays in Major League Baseball and competes in the National League. The football team would be in the National Football League and wouldn't have a "National League Championship Series."

So I'll proceed with the baseball team - the St. Louis Cardinals (wd:Q504309).

Now I need to find information about the championship series that the Cardinals won. Specifically, I need to identify:
1. The NLCS (National League Championship Series) wins
2. The World Series wins
3. The games played in those series where summaries are available

Let me first search for the properties related to championship series that the Cardinals might have won:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. points for (wd:P1358 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championship points"), also known as "championship points", "competition points", "league points", "pf", "points", etc.:
  - number of points in a league table or decathlon. (Use P1351 for goals/points in a match)
2. Championat ID (wd:P10800 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "championat.com/"), also known as "championat.com/", "Championat.com ID", "Championat.com identifier", "Championat identifier", "https://www.championat.com/", etc.:
  - identifier of a team, sports facility, or player on the Championat website
3. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "champion"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
4. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "tournament"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
5. time period (wd:P2348 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sports season"), also known as "epoch", "era", "historical period", "historic era", "historic period", etc.:
  - time period (historic period or era, sports season, theatre ... in which the subject occurred or with which it is associated
6. competition class (wd:P2094 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "class for competition", "compclass", "disability sport classifications", "qualification class", "qualifies for", etc.:
  - official classification by a regulating body under which the ... teams, participants, or equipment) qualifies for inclusion
7. sports season of league or competition (wd:P3450 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sport season"), also known as "athletics meeting item belonging to this instance", "edition of annual athletics meeting", "is an edition of athletics meeting", "is a season of", "is season of", etc.:
  - property that shows the competition of which the item is a season. Use P5138 for "season of club or team".
8. tournament format (wd:P8127 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "tournament system":
  - format of a sports tournament
9. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
10. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":
  - competition or sports event won by the subject

content.message:

content.message:
Perfect! I now have all the properties I need:

- The St. Louis Cardinals entity (wd:Q504309)
- The "competition won" property (P2522) to find championship series wins
- The "number of matches played" property (P1350) to count games played
- The specific championship series: National League Championship Series and World Series

Now I need to find the actual series that the Cardinals won. Let me search for the properties related to winning the NLCS and World Series:

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:

10. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p, matched via "commencement date"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
